title: Targeted tracer injection with online sensor
abstract: The invention is a method, a system, tools for use by the system, and an interpretation method for injecting and detecting tracers and conducting flow characterizing of a petroleum well. The method describes monitoring of travel time and slip velocity between two/three different phases (oil/water and possibly gas) in the well. The travel time and slip velocity are determined using an injection too for injection of an over pressurized injection of the partitioning tracers each of which would follow certain phase. The tracers are detected by an optical detection probe in the pipe. The slip velocity is obtained from the difference of travel time of two tracers which partition to two different phases.
claims: 1. A method of multi-phase petroleum well flow characterisation comprising, for at least two injection positions, along the well, the well having a local production flow of target fluids water and hydrocarbon fluids (oil and/or gas) at each position, whereof at least one or more zonal positions are along the production zone; and a) for at least two of the positions; using locally arranged injection devices; injecting in a synchronized manner, at least one set of at least a water-affine and a hydrocarbon-affine tracer; the tracers being optically detectable; injecting the at least one set of tracer into and/or through all phases in the local production flow simultaneously allowing local mixing of the phases; b) allowing transport of the production flow from the downhole injection points to a detector in the production flow at a detection point downstream of all the injection points; c) at the detection point, determining arrival times of the tracers; d) characterizing the flow behaviour of the well based on the arrival times of the tracers.
